CITY OF CARLSBAD
1200 CARLSBAD VILLAGE DRIVE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008-1989
(619) 434-2891
FAX: (619) 434-8367

RONALD R. BALL
CITY ATTORNEY

KAREN J. HIRATA
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

September 18, 1992

Jill Stecher, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

RE: LETTER NO. 92-418

Dear Ms. Stecher:

This will confirm our telephone conversation of Thursday, September 17, 1992 wherein I provided you with further information based on my discussions with Council Member Stanton. Council Member Stanton confirmed that Section 18702.2(f) of the Commission's regulations applies to Jazzercise, Inc. After a thorough discussion of the materiality regulations with Council Member Stanton, she feels there is little likelihood that the decision before the Council will meet the applicable materiality tests set forth in Commission regulation 18702.2(c)(1), (2) or (3).

As you and I previously discussed, the nature of the decision that will come before the City Council is whether or not to lobby NCTD, another governmental agency, as to the location of the train station. The actual decision regarding the location of the train station lies with NCTD which possess the power of eminent domain. (Pub.Util.Code §125240). No specific date has been set for this future Council decision and such date will depend, in part, upon a response, if any, from the NCTD board of directors to the Council's previous position taken on this matter (without Council Member Stanton's participation). We do not, however, anticipate such a decision within the next month.

We look forward to receiving your written opinion and, in the meantime, should you have any questions regarding the above, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

RONALD R. BALL
City Attorney

rmh

C: Council Member Stanton


[illegible]

CITY OF CARLSBAD
1200 CARLSBAD VILLAGE DRIVE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008-1989
(619) 434-2891
FAX: (619) 434-8367

RONALD R. BALL
CITY ATTORNEY

KAREN J. HIRATA
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

August 20, 1992

FPPC

AUG24 1992

Jill Stecher, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

RE: LETTER NO. 92-418

Dear Ms. Stecher:

As we discussed in our telephone conversation of August 11, 1992
regarding this matter, I have obtained a copy of the lease for the
real property at 2808 Roosevelt Street, Carlsbad, California. The
lease is held by Jazzercrize, Inc. and Council Member Stantons has no
interest in it. It commenced on January 1990 for five years at a
rental of approximately $11,000 per month which is indexed
according to the consumer price index. There is no percentage rent
contained in the lease based on sales or any other revenue factor.

As we additionally discussed, the decision that will come before
the City Council in the future, is whether or not to lobby NCTD,
another governmental agency, as to this location. The power and
decision to locate the commuter rail site lies with NCTD which
possesses, among others, the power of eminent domain.
(Pub.Util.Code §125240).

Finally, as you suggested, I have reviewed the advice letter to
Vincent F. Biondo, Jr. in your letter of April 22, 1991 (Advice
Letter No. A-91-054) and feel this request is considerably
different because the decision before the City Council is not
whether or not to locate a commuter rail station but whether or not
to comment on that location to NCTD.

I have copies of the lease in my files which I will be happy to
provide for you if you feel it is necessary.

Please give me a call to discuss this additional information once
you have had a chance to review it.

I appreciate your prompt attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
RONALD R. BALL
City Attorney

rmh

c:  Council Member Stanton

32   CITY OF CARLSBAD
1200 CARLSBAD VILLAGE DRIVE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008-1989
(619) 434-2891
FAX NO. (619) 434-8367

RONALD R. BALL
CITY ATTORNEY

KAREN J. HIRATA
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

July 13, 1992


Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Assistant General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804-0807

RE:  REQUEST FOR FORMAL ASSISTANCE PURSUANT TO COMMISSION
     REGULATION 18329 IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 83114(b)

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Carlsbad City Council Member Margaret Stanton requests your opinion
as to whether or not she may participate in decisions concerning
the proposed commuter rail station as described below.  She has
authorized me to request formal written advice on her behalf.  Her
mailing address is 4407 Highland Drive, Carlsbad, CA 92008.

North County Transit District (NCTD) proposes a commuter rail
system to be operated throughout San Diego County.  The commuter
rail system will have stations throughout the county and two of the
proposed stations will be located in Carlsbad.   NCTD recently
committed to pay 90.51 million dollars for the necessary right-of-
way for development of the project throughout San Diego County.
The plans call for 24 commuter stations for trains and buses.  The
commuter rail station in question is located in the area of Grand
Avenue and Washington Street in downtown Carlsbad.  This proposed
commuter rail station is estimated to cost approximately 2.1
million dollars paid for by NCTD.

Council Member Stanton is employed as the executive vice president
of Jazzercise, Inc.  She is also a member of the board of directors
and a corporate officer.  Jazzercise, Inc. leases office space at
2808 Roosevelt Street over 300 feet but less than 2500 feet from
the proposed commuter rail station as shown on the attached map.
The lease term has three years remaining plus an option for renewal
of an additional five years.   Council Member Stanton receives
income from Jazzercise, Inc. exceeding $250 per year.  Most of the
revenue earned by Jazzercise, Inc results from its operation of
approximately 4500 franchises worldwide.  In addition to its world
wide franchises, the corporation operates a mail-order and retail
store, a television production company and a studio at its

Roosevelt location. It also subleases a part of these offices to
several of its local franchisees for dance fitness classes. It is
not known whether or not the railway center will have an economic
impact on these operations.

Jazzercise, Inc. is wholly owned by Judi Sheppard Missett. She and
her husband are exploring the possibility of purchasing the real
property located at 2742 State Street which is shown on the
attached map. If purchased, this property will become an asset of
Jazzercise, Inc. or one of its related companies.

The decision before the Housing and Redevelopment Commission will
be whether or not to continue to cooperate with NCTD regarding the
location of the station. The decision will not change the legally
allowable use of Jazzercise's leased property nor is it reasonably
foreseeable that Jazzercise, Inc. will change the actual use of the
property as a result of this decision. Since the rail station is
located more than 300 feet from the leased property it is not
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will result in a changed
use of the leased property. It is not anticipated that the
decision will increase or decrease the amount of rent paid by
Jazzercise, Inc. nor will it result in a change in the termination
date of the lease.

Council Member Stanton respectfully requests your opinion for her
guidance as to whether or not she may participate in this decision.

Should you need additional information, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

RONALD R. BALL
City Attorney
